name: handoff-retention
question: Which handoff briefs older than the configured TTL exist, and which are KEEP-pinned for permanent retention?

schema.swept: array of {file, swept_at, age_days, brief_first_line}; in default mode lists would-be-swept candidates with swept_at:null; in --gc mode lists actually-swept entries with swept_at:ISO-8601
schema.retained_keep_pinned: array of strings (paths exempt via sibling .KEEP file)
schema.retained_under_ttl: array of {file, age_days} (briefs younger than threshold)
schema.briefing: string (frozen-format briefing line; empty string when no drift, suppresses session-start line)

_swept_entry_schema.swept_at: string|null (ISO-8601 UTC; null in default-mode listing)
_swept_entry_schema.brief_first_line: string (first non-empty line of the brief, ~200 chars max, JSON-escaped)

fallback: Read .claude/handoff/ directly; for each handoff-*.md file, check sibling <file>.KEEP for exemption, compute age in days from mtime, compare to (handoffRetention.threshold from .claude/config.json, default 30 days). Before any delete, append a JSON line summarizing the file to .claude/handoff/_summary.jsonl.

gcMode.summary: Handoff retention sweep (SWEEP-01). Sweeps .claude/handoff/handoff-*.md for files where age_days > threshold (default 30) AND no sibling <file>.KEEP exists. Before each delete, appends a JSON entry to _summary.jsonl preserving file, swept_at, age_days, brief_first_line (audit trail preserved per SWEEP-08).

cheap.behavior: Read-only at --gc-cheap (briefing only, per SWEEP-01). Silent on success; never blocks the session-start briefing. Drift-surfacing is delegated to default-mode iteration-loop pickup (typical_sessions_using = 'every'); this --gc-cheap mode exists for symmetry with archive-retention's hook wiring and as a future-extension point.
cheap.exit_codes: Always 0 (graceful). Internal failures are logged to stderr only.

on_demand.behavior: Destructive sweep with no time budget. Writes JSON line to _summary.jsonl BEFORE each delete (SWEEP-08 invariant). Reports a single-line JSON summary on stdout: {swept, before, after, threshold, thresholdSource, summarized, sweptFiles?}.

summary_schema.summarized: integer (entries appended to _summary.jsonl; equals swept on clean runs)
summary_schema.sweptFiles: string (comma-separated basenames deleted; omitted if empty)

gcMode.criteria: Filename matches /^handoff-.+\.md$/ AND no sibling <file>.KEEP exists AND mtime is older than (now - threshold). Default threshold: 30 days (DEC-54). KEEP-pinned files are NEVER swept regardless of age (HANDOFF-01 substrate consumed verbatim).
gcMode.action: 1) Read first non-empty line of brief (brief_first_line). 2) Append JSON line to .claude/handoff/_summary.jsonl (atomic append). 3) Verify summary write succeeded (read-back last line). 4) rm the file. Summary write must succeed before delete; on summary write failure, the file is NOT deleted (SWEEP-08 Probandurgy invariant — never delete without recovery surface).
gcMode.atomic_semantics: Per-file delete sequence is sequential and uses POSIX-atomic append for _summary.jsonl (each entry < PIPE_BUF / 4KB, atomic across concurrent appenders). No file-level lock acquired -- concurrent sweeps from different sessions are race-tolerant: each sweep reads files that exist at its scan time; if two sweeps target the same file, the second's rm becomes a no-op (idempotent). _summary.jsonl may receive duplicate entries in the rare double-sweep case, which is acceptable (audit trail favors over-recording).
gcMode.threshold_config: Configurable via .claude/config.json key 'handoffRetention.threshold' (numeric days OR ISO-8601 duration string PnD). Falls back to default 30 days if config absent or malformed.

provenance.note: Caps unbounded growth of .claude/handoff/handoff-*.md by sweeping files older than 30 days (DEC-54 surface-specific TTL). Mirrors archive-retention's --gc / --gc-cheap pattern with three differences: (a) operates on individual files instead of dirs; (b) 30d default TTL, not 90d (handoffs are routing artifacts, value decays faster); (c) sibling <file>.KEEP convention (HANDOFF-01) instead of in-dir KEEP file. Pre-delete _summary.jsonl preserves recoverable audit trail per SWEEP-08 Probandurgy invariant. Closes DISC-HYGIENE-03 (retention-without-sweep asymmetry — Hygiene Arcs 1+2 defined retention/KEEP-pin but no sweep mechanism).
